subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – Rani Abbakka
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Rani Abbakka was a brave queen of Ullal in Karnataka. She is remembered for her courage and leadership in defending her kingdom against Portuguese invaders. Her determination inspired her people to resist foreign rule.
questions: Who was Rani Abbakka?
Which kingdom did she rule?
What is she remembered for?
Who did she defend her kingdom against?
How did she inspire her people?

passages.paragraph: From a young age, Abbakka was trained in self-defense, sword fighting, and archery. Her education included military skills, which later helped her lead armies and plan strategic defenses.
questions: What skills was Abbakka trained in?
Why was she trained in military skills?
What weapons did she learn to use?
How did her training help her later?
What kind of leader did her training prepare her to be?

passages.paragraph: The Portuguese wanted to capture Ullal and expand their territory. Abbakka refused to surrender and organized her army to fight back. Her bravery made it difficult for the invaders to defeat her.
questions: Who wanted to capture Ullal?
What did Abbakka refuse to do?
How did she respond to the invaders?
What did she organize?
Why was it difficult for the Portuguese to defeat her?

passages.paragraph: Abbakka used clever strategies to defend her kingdom. She made use of forts, natural barriers, and surprise attacks. Her ability to plan effectively ensured the safety of Ullal for many years.
questions: What strategies did Abbakka use?
What defenses did she make use of?
How did she use surprise attacks?
What was the result of her planning?
How long was Ullal kept safe?

passages.paragraph: She was not only a warrior but also a wise ruler. Abbakka cared for her people, settled disputes fairly, and ensured that the kingdom was prosperous despite constant threats.
questions: Was Abbakka only a warrior?
How did she treat her people?
How did she settle disputes?
What did she ensure for her kingdom?
How did she manage prosperity under threats?

passages.paragraph: Abbakka’s courage inspired neighboring rulers and people. Many joined her cause against the Portuguese, showing that leadership and bravery can unite communities for a common goal.
questions: Who was inspired by Abbakka?
Why did people join her cause?
Who did she oppose?
What does her leadership demonstrate?
How did bravery affect the people?

passages.paragraph: Despite facing betrayal from some allies, Abbakka remained determined. She carefully chose trustworthy generals and ensured that her army stayed loyal and strong.
questions: Did Abbakka face betrayal?
How did she respond to betrayal?
Whom did she choose carefully?
How did she ensure loyalty?
Why was her army strong?

passages.paragraph: Rani Abbakka’s legacy lives on in history as a symbol of resistance and courage. Her story teaches that determination, intelligence, and bravery can overcome powerful opponents.
questions: What does Abbakka’s legacy symbolize?
What lessons does her story teach?
What qualities helped her succeed?
Who did she overcome?
Why is her story remembered today?

passages.paragraph: She motivated women and men alike to take part in the defense of their homeland. Her actions showed that leadership is not limited by gender, and courage can come from anyone.
questions: Whom did Abbakka motivate?
What did she encourage people to do?
What did her actions show about leadership?
Does gender limit courage according to her example?
Who can show bravery according to her story?

passages.paragraph: Rani Abbakka is celebrated for her patriotism, tactical intelligence, and determination. Her life reminds us that standing up for one’s beliefs and protecting one’s people is the mark of a great leader.
questions: Why is Rani Abbakka celebrated?
What qualities made her a great leader?
What does her life remind us?
What does standing up for one’s beliefs show?
How did she protect her people?
